simple: A group of representatives who cast the official votes for president in the USA.
full: A body of 538 electors established by the US Constitution, which forms every four years for the sole purpose of electing the president and vice president of the United States.

simple: First Past The Post - the candidate with the most votes wins.
full: An electoral system in which voters cast a vote for a single candidate, and the candidate with the most votes is elected, regardless of whether they have a majority.
